llmWorkflowPrompt.purpose: Re-execute the repo's current workflow for producing the Auctionomics Remotion video whenever the source research documents change. Follow the existing architecture, keep the work deterministic, and regenerate the full Remotion artifact chain, including audio commentary, when the authoritative source documents are updated.
llmWorkflowPrompt.role: You are a coding assistant operating inside this repository. Your job is to read the current source documents, update the Auctionomics project implementation under projects/auctionomics/video/ plus the shared Remotion app under video/ when needed, update or regenerate the narration assets, run validation, and leave the repo in a reviewable state with a current rendered artifact.

file.role: selective source for closing asks, gating questions, and management-call framing

sourceUpdateTrigger.rule: Treat projects/auctionomics/sources/investment-committee-memo.md and projects/auctionomics/sources/management-diligence-request-list.md as the two root inputs that govern the entire derived artifact chain.
If either file changes materially, rerun the complete workflow end to end rather than only patching isolated files.

coreWorkflow.principle: Use projects/auctionomics/sources/investment-committee-memo.md and projects/auctionomics/sources/management-diligence-request-list.md exactly as they exist in the repo at the time of execution. Treat them as the authoritative inputs. Do not rewrite them or supplement them with outside research unless the user explicitly asks.
Do not build or maintain a generic Markdown-to-slides system for this repo. The workflow is manual editorial curation from prose into typed scene data.

Treat projects/auctionomics/video/data/scenes.ts as the primary implementation of the current narrative. Update that data first, then update composition/components only if the new content requires it.
Keep the video institutional, restrained, high-signal, and deterministic. This is an investor memo in motion, not a product promo.

Preserve the established visual direction: deep slate or warm off-black backgrounds, muted gold or copper accents, restrained typography, and low-amplitude motion. Prefer investor-research tone over startup-marketing tone.
Preserve the shared Remotion app under video/ and the Auctionomics project code under projects/auctionomics/video/. Update in place; do not rebuild the app from scratch.

This workflow includes audio commentary. Use Kokoro on CPU, keep narration scene-based, store narration inputs in projects/auctionomics/video/data/narration.json, generate one WAV per scene under projects/auctionomics/video/public/audio/, and use the mandated voice af_bella.

Use UV-managed Python tooling for narration generation. Use uv sync and the repo's pnpm test:shared, pnpm project:audio -- --project auctionomics, pnpm project:dev -- --project auctionomics, pnpm project:render -- --project auctionomics, and pnpm project:verify -- --project auctionomics commands as the standard execution path. Run pnpm test:shared first for fast shared regression coverage and treat pnpm project:verify -- --project auctionomics as the final real-project acceptance gate.

idempotenceRules.rule: Start by inspecting the current repo state, including uncommitted changes. Never revert unrelated user edits.
Compare the latest source documents against the current scene data and update only the scenes, citations, and layout details that are materially affected.

If the current implementation already reflects the source documents closely enough and the generated audio files plus final MP4 are current and present, make no source edits. Report a no-op result rather than churning formatting, copy, audio, or renders.
Keep stable file names, scene identifiers, component names, and composition IDs unless a change is genuinely required by the updated narrative.
Prefer editing existing files over creating new abstractions. Add new files or components only when the current structure cannot cleanly support the revised content.
Do not modify the source memo or diligence list unless the user explicitly asks you to rewrite those documents.
If either primary source document changed materially, regenerate the entire artifact chain after updating code: narration data, scene WAV files, and the final rendered MP4.
For source-driven reruns, completion requires all derived artifacts to be brought back into sync with the updated sources.

scene.purpose: Establish partnership, date, recommendation, and the gating issues immediately.
Frame the market-design thesis and why the combination of Auctionomics and OneChronos matters.
scene.delivery: Text-led thesis framing with concise supporting points on each entity and the product.

scene.purpose: Show visible platform, team, and funding signal while making clear the compute market is still an announcement.
scene.delivery: Timeline or milestone layout that emphasizes dated proof points and the traction gap.

scene.purpose: Explain the competitive field, the structural-edge argument, and the timing risk.
scene.delivery: Structured context view with short explanatory captions.

source.section: Core risk #1: partnership structure, governance, and durability

scene.purpose: Show the partnership-governance gating issue: legal form, IP ownership, and Milgrom's commitment.
scene.delivery: Risk-panel treatment with highlighted unknowns and partnership-durability questions.

scene.purpose: Show the regulatory-pathway gating issue: forward classification, ATS scope, and competitor contrast.

source.section: Core risk #3: market-design feasibility for compute
Core risk #4: market formation, competitive timing, and traction

scene.purpose: Show market-design feasibility and traction gaps: untested auction, no participants, chicken-and-egg problem.
scene.delivery: Risk-panel treatment emphasizing missing proof and the execution gap.

scene.purpose: Close on the recommendation and present the hardest next-step asks for management.
scene.delivery: Closing recommendation plus selective diligence asks.

finalResponseContract.item: Summarize the source changes you detected.
State explicitly whether either root source file changed materially and whether that triggered a full workflow rerun.
List the files you changed and why.
Report whether the run was a true update or an intentional no-op.
Report whether you regenerated the full artifact chain or only refreshed missing or stale artifacts, and why.
Report the narration and audio outputs you generated.
Report the validation commands you ran and their outcomes.
Call out any remaining review risks, especially around editorial judgment or timing.
